sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 5 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, DOWN, DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 64, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Level 2: charcoal C-shape at rows 18-29, cols 27-35 (after probe RIGHT+DOWN). - Yellow C-shape at rows 42-53, cols 3-17 (target). - RIGHT moves charcoal right by 6 cols. DOWN had no visible effect on charcoal. - Need to move charcoal LEFT ~18 cols and DOWN ~24 rows to overlap with yellow.
- Plan: Try 3 LEFT to align horizontally, then DOWN steps to align vertically.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 7 of about 50 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting findings:
1. After 3x LEFT: charcoal moved from cols 27-35 to cols 9-17 (horizontally aligned with yellow!)
2. After 4x DOWN: charcoal stayed at cols 9-17, rows 18-29 (DOWN had NO effect on charcoal!)
So DOWN doesn't move the charcoal at all. The charcoal can only move horizontally (LEFT/RIGHT).
But the yellow shape is at rows 42-53, and the charcoal is at rows 18-29. They're at different vertical positions and can't overlap.
Wait, maybe the goal is different in Level 2. Let me reconsider.
Looking at the image:
- Charcoal C-shape at top-left (rows 18-29, cols 3-17)
- Yellow C-shape at bottom-left (rows 42-53, cols 3-17)
- Black shape with dots at top-right (rows 18-29, cols 45-59)
The charcoal and yellow shapes are now horizontally aligned (both at cols 3-17), but at different vertical positions.
Maybe the goal is to move the BLACK shape to overlap with the yellow shape? The black shape has dots, and when it overlaps with the yellow shape, the dots turn yellow.
But the black shape is at rows 18-29, cols 45-59, and the yellow shape is at rows 42-53, cols 3-17. They're far apart.
Wait, maybe LEFT/RIGHT moves BOTH the charcoal and the black shape. In Level 1, LEFT moved the charcoal right and the black left. Let me check if the black shape moved after the 3 LEFT actions.
From the segmentation after 3x LEFT:
- Black: boundary=[[18, 45], [18, 53], ...] (same as initial)
So the black shape didn't move after 3 LEFT actions. But in Level 1, LEFT moved both shapes.
Hmm, maybe in Level 2, the movement mechanics are different. Let me check if the black shape moved at all.
Actually, looking at the output more carefully, after 3x LEFT, the black boundary is [[18, 45], [18, 53], ...], which is the same as the initial. So the black didn't move.
But wait, in Level 1, pressing LEFT moved the charcoal right and the black left. In Level 2, pressing LEFT moved the charcoal left (from cols 27-35 to cols 9-17). So LEFT moves the charcoal in the LEFT direction in Level 2, but in the RIGHT direction in Level 1.
This is confusing. Let me re-examine Level 1:
- In Level 1, the charcoal was at cols 0-8 and the black was at cols 54-62.
- Pressing LEFT moved the charcoal to cols 3-11 (right) and the black to cols 51-59 (left).
- So LEFT moved the charcoal RIGHT and the black LEFT.
In Level 2:
- The charcoal is at cols 21-29 (initially) and the black is at cols 45-59.
- Pressing RIGHT moved the charcoal to cols 27-35 (right).
- Pressing LEFT moved the charcoal to cols 9-17 (left).
- So RIGHT moves the charcoal RIGHT and LEFT moves the charcoal LEFT.
This is different from Level 1! In Level 1, LEFT moved the charcoal RIGHT. In Level 2, LEFT moves the charcoal LEFT.
So the movement direction depends on the level or the relative positions of the shapes.
OK so in Level 2:
- LEFT moves charcoal LEFT
- RIGHT moves charcoal RIGHT
- DOWN doesn't move charcoal
- UP might not move charcoal either
The charcoal can only move horizontally. The yellow shape is at a different vertical position, so they can't overlap.
Maybe the goal is not to overlap the charcoal with the yellow. Maybe the goal is to match the charcoal's horizontal position with the yellow's horizontal position, and then press SPACE.
Currently, the charcoal is at cols 9-17 (top bar) and the yellow is at cols 9-17 (top bar). They're horizontally aligned!
Let me try pressing SPACE now.
Wait, but the charcoal and yellow are at different vertical positions (rows 18-29 vs 42-53). How can they be "aligned" if they're at different rows?
Maybe the goal is just to have the charcoal's horizontal position match the yellow's horizontal position, regardless of vertical position. Let me try SPACE.
Actually, let me also check if the black shape has moved. The black shape is at cols 45-59. If LEFT moves the charcoal LEFT, maybe it also moves the black RIGHT (opposite direction).
After 3x LEFT:
- Charcoal: cols 9-17 (moved left by 18 cols)
- Black: cols 45-59 (unchanged)
So the black didn't move. This is different from Level 1 where both shapes moved.
Hmm, maybe in Level 2, only the charcoal moves (not the black). Or maybe the black is blocked by something.
Let me try SPACE now that the charcoal is horizontally aligned with the yellow.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- Level 2: charcoal C-shape at rows 18-29, cols 9-17 (after 3 LEFT).
- Yellow C-shape at rows 42-53, cols 9-17 (target).
- LEFT/RIGHT move charcoal horizontally (6 cols per step). DOWN has no effect.
- Charcoal and yellow are now horizontally aligned (both cols 9-17).
- Black shape with dots at rows 18-29, cols 45-59 (unchanged).
- Goal: horizontally align charcoal with yellow, then press SPACE.
Plan: Press SPACE since charcoal and yellow are now horizontally aligned.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Charcoal and yellow are horizontally aligned at cols 9-17
# Try SPACE to complete the level
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"SPACE: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}, board_changed={result.get('board_changed')}")

if not result.get('level_completed'):
    # SPACE didn't work, try moving black shape
    # Check if LEFT/RIGHT affects black shape
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'B' and 50 < n['pixels'] < 100:
            print(f"Black: boundary={n['boundary']}")
    
    # Try moving black shape with LEFT/RIGHT
    result = action(['LEFT'])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'B' and 50 < n['pixels'] < 100:
            print(f"After LEFT, Black: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] > 50:
            print(f"After LEFT, Charcoal: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
